questions.question: What is the key behavioral difference between `watch` and `watchEffect` regarding when they first run?
options: `watch` runs immediately; `watchEffect` is lazy by default
`watchEffect` runs immediately; `watch` is lazy by default
Both are lazy: neither runs until a dependency changes

questions.question: Which composable provides both `newVal` and `oldVal` in its callback?
Why might `watchEffect` miss a reactive dependency in an async callback?
options: Async functions disable dependency tracking globally
Only the first `await` statement is tracked
Vue stops tracking dependencies after the first `await` yields control
`watchEffect` doesn't support async callbacks at all
